meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Git with AI: Master SaaS Development for Students
content.seo_description: Overcome the lack of SaaS knowledge with this expert Git with AI checklist. Master Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first professional-grade project.
content.answer_block: Bridging the gap from student to SaaS developer requires mastering version control alongside AI agents. By integrating Git workflows with Antigravity and Claude Code, you automate boilerplate, enforce architectural patterns, and transform complex documentation into executable code, effectively bypassing the steep learning curve of traditional, manual full-stack development.
content.intro:
The blank editor stares back, a silent testament to the 'SaaS gap'—that paralyzing space between knowing how to code and knowing how to build a production-ready application. You have the ambition, but the architecture, deployment, and version control feel like a labyrinth designed to keep students out.

But the rules of the game have changed. By leveraging AI-native development tools, you can now collapse weeks of manual configuration into hours of high-leverage engineering. This isn't just about writing code; it's about orchestrating agents to manage your Git history, ensuring your codebase remains as robust as a senior engineer's project.

steps.title: Architecting the SaaS Skeleton
steps.description: Before writing logic, you must define the directory structure and tech stack. Use Antigravity to generate a production-grade folder structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, scalable Next.js project structure with Tailwind CSS and Supabase integration. Include a 'lib', 'hooks', and 'components' directory.

steps.title: Context-Aware Git Initialization
steps.description: Never commit 'wip' again. Use Claude Code to ensure your initial commit follows semantic versioning and industry-standard formatting.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional .gitignore and a README.md that outlines the MVP features of this SaaS project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'feat: initialize project structure with supabase and tailwind'

steps.title: Automating Feature Branches
steps.description: Isolate your development. Create branches for specific features and use AI to document the intent of every change before it hits the repo.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a technical specification for an authentication module using Supabase Auth. List the necessary files and functions.

steps.title: AI-Assisted Code Review
steps.description: Before merging, run a review process where the AI acts as a senior engineer, checking for security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for security flaws, specifically focusing on Supabase RLS policies and API key exposure.

steps.title: Deployment Pipeline Orchestration
steps.description: Sync your Git workflow with your deployment environment to ensure every merge triggers a staging build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Github Actions workflow file that runs tests and builds the Next.js app on every push to main.

pro_tips: Always define a 'System Prompt' for your AI agent that reflects your project's coding standards to maintain consistency across files.
Use Git hooks to automatically trigger Claude Code linting before allowing any commit to be pushed to your repository.
Treat your Git commit history as a documentation log; use AI to summarize complex refactors into human-readable changelogs.
Never hardcode environment variables; use AI to generate .env.example files and validate them against your schema during build time.
When stuck, use the 'diff' feature in Claude Code to compare your current implementation against a known-good boilerplate pattern.
resources: Git Best Practices for SaaS: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: Once your repository is configured, deploy your initial build to Vercel and share the public link in our community forum for a peer code review.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact blueprint to bypass the 'SaaS gap' and build like a pro. Stop struggling with configuration and start shipping value. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
